title: July 2025 Newsletter
text: Enjoy Your Latest Cavallo News - Your Cavallo Monthly: Hoof-Saving Hacks, Hero Horses & Behind-the-Scenes Buzz! Dog and Pony Show... At the Airport? Mini Therapy Ponies Calm Before-Flight Jitters! Hold onto your boarding passes—Vancouver Airport just turned the terminal into a mini-fairytale! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t4mpzTYVRCQ Green Acres Therapy Horses has teamed up with Vancouver International Airport (YVR) to bring adorable therapy ponies to socialize with weary travellers, making it the first airport in North America to include miniature horses in its animal-assisted stress-relief program. Normally a role held by canines, Magic and Tinkerbell, two Cavallo-booted therapy ponies, hoofed their way into YVR’s “Less Airport Stress Initiative” (LASI) and are now helping travellers feel calm and connected in an otherwise stressful environment. This dinky dynamic duo rocks the cutest in hoof fashion— Cavallo CLB “Cute Little Boots” ! Not only do they protect tiny hooves from busy terminal floors, they add exceptional flair to the job. So, Next time you fly out of YVR, Magic and Tinkerbell may just be waiting in the terminal, ready to add some pony-powered Zen to your journey! title: Cavallo LEB Long Ear Boot User Manual
h1: Cavallo LEB "Long Ear Boots" User Manual
text: Cavallo LEB "Long Ear Boots" User Manual The Cavallo LEB Long Ear Boot is the only Hoof Boot designed specifically for donkeys. Whether you’re already familiar with hoof boots or exploring them for the first time, choosing the LEB means your donkey is stepping into comfort, protection, and freedom of movement. Donkeys aren’t just small, long-eared horses - their hooves are narrower, more upright, with strong frogs and large heel bulbs. That’s why the LEB isn’t a modified horse boot, but a purpose-built design created from the ground up to match your donkey’s unique anatomy. Developed with input from leading donkey trimmers and hoof care professionals, the LEB offers a secure, supportive fit that respects natural hoof function. It reflects a philosophy rooted in barefoot wellness—supporting healthy movement without pain or restriction—and incorporates decades of real-world feedback from equine caretakers worldwide. This manual is your complete guide to success. Inside, you’ll find everything needed to measure, fit, break in, and care for your LEBs—plus practical tips, troubleshooting advice, and bonus insights from other devoted donkey owners who’ve already made the switch. Table of Contents What are the Cavallo LEB Long Ear Boots? Understanding Donkey Hoof Health & the Barefoot Advantage How to Measure and Size for Your Donkey How to Put On and Take Off Cavallo LEB Boots Fittin

title: Why Cavallo Hoof Boots are the Best for Therapy, Rehab & Turnout
text: Why Cavallo Hoof Boots are the Best for Therapy, Rehab & Turnout Laminitis and Founder, thin soles, Navicular Syndrome, hoof puncture wounds, abscesses, White Line Disease, Trush, hoof cracks, sole bruising & soft tissue injuries, contracted heels, ringbone, sidebone and joint inflammation/Arthritis are all hoof issues that can be helped by using Cavallo Boots during healing. Whether barefoot or shod, horses all around the world with Laminitis and other hoof issues are experiencing great success using Cavallo Hoof Boots for comfort, healing, hoof therapy, rehabilitation and turnout. Immediate Relief The mental well-being of your equine is as important as his physical well-being. When pain is alleviated for a horse with Laminitis or any other painful hoof issue, your horse is happier and able to move more. Movement stimulates blood flow, and the nourishing blood is then able to work its way to the problem areas to facilitate faster, more effective healing. Cavallo Hoof Boots have technically advanced shock-absorbing soles, which make every step predictable for your horse (they stop anticipating that a step could hurt and start to move more freely). The soles absorb the concussive forces that would otherwise refer through the hoof. The result is an immediate difference in the horse’s willingness to move. title: August 2025 Newsletter
text: Enjoy Your Latest Cavallo News - Full of Hoof-Saving Hacks & Tips to Keep your Horse Brilliantly Booted! Long Ears to the Ground: Carole Talks Donkey Boots on the Humble Hoof Podcast! We’ve always known that donkeys need and deserve the same comfort and protection as horses—but their hooves are anything but the same! Narrower walls, stronger digital cushions, and higher, rounder heel bulbs mean that most “horse” boots simply don’t fit, often causing rubs or spinning on the hoof. That’s why we’ve been hard at work designing something special: the LEB (Cavallo Long-Ear Boot), created just for our long-eared friends! Curious about the journey behind this innovation? I recently joined Alicia of The Humble Hoof podcast to share more about donkey hooves and the story of the evolution of our new LEB Boot, arriving this Fall. Join Carole Here! So, your Vet Recommended Cavallos… What Happened Next? Veterinarians across the globe recommend Cavallo Hoof Boots to provide comfort and protection during hoof rehabilitation, and for long-term soundness. Hearing this feedback from the professionals who care for your horses is powerful—and we’d love for you to share your own story! By sharing your personal story about how Cavallos helped your horse, we can work together to inspire and guide other horse owners who are looking for trusted, vet-approved solutions. title: Your Cavallo News - October, 2025
h1: Enjoy Your Latest Cavallo News - Full of Hoof-Saving Hacks & Tips to Keep your Horse Brilliantly Booted!
text: Enjoy Your Latest Cavallo News - Full of Hoof-Saving Hacks & Tips to Keep your Horse Brilliantly Booted! Happy 'Hoof-o-ween' from Cavallo!???? From Pain to Progress: Supporting Equines on Their Laminitis Journey https://www.youtube.com/shorts/m2MkznNBrjk?feature=sharecontrols=0 Laminitis affects a significant number of equines worldwide… It's possible that as many as one in ten horses will experience an episode during their lifetime. This painful inflammation within the hoof can make even small movements difficult… Yet gentle, supported movement is one of the most effective ways to aid recovery. This video demonstrates how well-fitted Cavallos can make a dramatic difference in comfort and healing. For barefoot and shod horses and donkeys (and even zebras!), Cavallos provide cushioning, protection, and support that help horses stay mobile while minimizing concussion to the hoof. Continued movement encourages healthy circulation, allowing blood and essential nutrients to reach the damaged laminae — an important factor in recovery. Encouraging your horse or donkey to stay comfortably active under veterinary and farrier guidance can help promote natural healing and improve overall hoof health, giving your horse the best chance for a successful recovery. title: Your Cavallo News – August 2024
text: Your Cavallo News – August 2024 A Very Special Invitation Will be Sent to You Soon... You’ll be receiving a link to a special contest for a chance to win tickets to the 2024 Liberty Festival , along with a chance to personally meet Heartland’s Amber Marshall! The Cowboy and the Queen The Cowboy and The Queen is a fascinating documentary that chronicles the remarkable friendship between Monty Roberts and Queen Elizabeth II. Our friend Monty Roberts, an esteemed California horse trainer (now in his late 80s), revolutionized horse training with his gentle methods, which contrast sharply with traditional, more forceful techniques. His approach emphasizes cooperation and understanding rather than domination. In the late 1980s, Queen Elizabeth II became aware of Roberts’ innovative techniques and invited him to demonstrate his methods at Windsor Castle. Her admiration for Roberts’ approach led to a lasting friendship, and together they championed a more humane and effective way of training horses. Their friendship, as portrayed in this film, highlights their mutual respect and shared commitment to improving horse training practices worldwide, leaving a legacy that continues to influence equestrian methods to this day. The Cowboy and the Queen is in theatres now and will be streaming on MasterClass this September. Watch The Cowboy and the Queen Trailer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v

title: Your Cavallo News – September 2024
text: Your Cavallo News – September 2024 Step Into the Future with the World's Most Intelligent Hoof Boot! We are so excited to be in the final design stages of the world’s first smart boot. This is truly a game changer, which will allow you to understand your horse’s health and well-being in real time through AI. Imagine viewing your horse’s gait, balance, ground force reaction and more on your own personal device. This diagnostic tool can identify early signs of lameness before they are even visible to the eye. The sensors are placed in the sole of Cavallo Hoof Boots, transforming it into the world’s first diagnostic hoof boot. Stay tuned for more news on Consteed! Guns Up - Boots On! title: NEW! TREK/BFB Colour Pop Hoof Boot Straps Pack
text: Mix. Match. Make It YOURS. Add a Pop of Colour to Your Cavallo Trek (and BFB) Hoof Boots! Is your horse a true individual? Elevate your Cavallo Trek or BFB Hoof Boots with the Colour Pop Straps Pack! This pack is a vibrant collection of 3 pairs of durable hoof boot straps in hunter orange, bright yellow and turquoise. These exchangeable hoof boot straps are designed to add a splash of fun while offering extra security for your horse's hoof boots - Trek/BFB Colour Pop straps let you switch up your horse’s look in seconds! Whether you're hitting the trails, competing, or just out for a ride, these colourful Trek/BFB straps not only look great, but help provide a secure fit to keep your boots in place, no matter the terrain. Why You NEED the Trek/BFB Colour Pop Straps Pack: The Cavallo Colour Pop Straps Pack is not only fun and stylish — it’s incredibly practical. The different strap colours make it easy to tell different hoof boots apart for different horses, especially in busy barns or shared tack rooms. They’re also perfect for riders who use different boot sizes on front and hind hooves, allowing you to instantly identify which boots go where. With bright, bold colours that stand out, Colour Pop straps help you stay organized, avoid mix-ups, and keep every ride simple and stress-free. Kiwi the Mini Horse and his lovely Cavallo-CLB-clad co-workers from Stampede of Love Mini Horse Therapy stood quietly and patiently along the road. The first moment of recognition from the travelling monks was a soft, delighted “oooh” from the monks’ flag bearer...and that said it all. There was no grand fanfare, just an unspoken meeting of kindred spirits - little hooves and gentle robes, presence and peace blending like a quiet prayer. In that instant, many hearts were touched. The monk, the onlookers, and everyone near enough to feel that the magic of kindness from equines can feel as vast as any sacred journey. This 2,300-mile pilgrimage spreading compassion, unity, and mindfulness from Texas to Washington, D.C.,has been joyously celebrated by every community they have passed through. title: Winter Hoof Care Tips for Horse Owners!
text: Winter Hoof Care Tips for Horse Owners! By your Cavallo Team Winter hoof care often feels like its own discipline! Not only are we dealing with frozen water troughs, muddy paddocks, icy pathways, and rock-hard footing, but we also have to consider how winter conditions affect our horses’ hooves. Once the ground freezes and moisture levels rise, minor issues can escalate quickly unless you stay ahead of them with a consistent routine. The Foundation Beneath Every Hoof A correct, regular trim is the foundation of all hoof care. Even though many horses work less in winter, their hooves continue to grow, and a longer trim cycle allows the capsule to creep forward. That shift affects breakover, alters loading patterns, and increases strain on internal structures, such as the deep digital flexor tendon and the laminae. Keeping trims on schedule helps maintain balance, preserves proper hoof mechanics, and supports more secure footing on unpredictable winter ground. Moisture: A Silent Saboteur of Hoof Health If you live in a wet climate, preventing thrush and white line disease becomes even more critical. Winter brings mud and slush , along with ice and snow, and cold weather doesn’t stop bacterial activity, especially when paddocks stay damp and muddy. Using a non-caustic antibacterial product regularly helps protect the frog and central sulcus without harming healthy tissue. Daily cl
